disclaimer: Axes and pro/con bullets are curated analysis (QVAC / local AI focus), not certified ratings. Verify against primary sources.

entities.name: QVAC (Tether)
entities.tagline: On-device intelligence: Workbench, vertical apps — not cloud-only gatekeepers.

facts.label: Positioning
facts.value: Tether’s answer to centralized AI: models and agents run locally; data stays on the device.

facts.label: Public surface
facts.value: Workbench (local AI app), Health, Translate — privacy-first; Genesis datasets & mobile tooling on the roadmap.

facts.label: Vs hyperscalers
facts.value: Vertically integrated Tether AI story: data sovereignty + agents on devices, not only hosted APIs.

facts.label: Value between agents
facts.value: When fleets of agents coordinate work, closing the loop on liquidity is a planning dimension alongside model delivery and compliance.

pros.text: Aligns privacy and ops control: avoids “prompts live in cloud” as the default, useful for enterprise and regulated contexts.
Clear product surface (Workbench + verticals) for roadmaps, dependencies, and adoption metrics.

cons.text: Younger ecosystem vs mature cloud platforms: integrations, enterprise support, and time-to-market need explicit planning.

entities.name: OpenAI (API cloud)
entities.tagline: Default for hosted assistants: fast shipping, vendor dependency.

facts.label: Model access
facts.value: Hosted APIs and partner programs — not on-prem inference by default.

facts.label: What they do “more” vs QVAC
facts.value: Tooling maturity, global latency, integrator ecosystem — at the cost of on-device data sovereignty.

pros.text: Operational reliability and speed for teams that need to ship immediately.

cons.text: Prompts and telemetry sit in vendor-controlled environments — not a local-first stack like QVAC.

entities.name: Ollama
entities.tagline: Open-weight models locally: developer-friendly, not Tether’s product suite.

facts.label: Focus
facts.value: Run LLMs locally with downloadable models; strong community and integrations.

facts.label: What it doesn’t bundle vs QVAC
facts.value: No unified Workbench/vertical roadmap or Tether-side agent micropayment vision.

pros.text: Wide developer adoption for local inference prototypes without model-vendor lock-in.

cons.text: Enterprise packaging and end-to-end “curated platform” story are often DIY — different from a guided stack.

entities.name: Bittensor
entities.tagline: Subnets and TAO: incentivized market for distributed intelligence.

facts.label: Token
facts.value: TAO; network economics and subnet-specific incentives.

facts.label: Vs QVAC
facts.value: Emphasizes protocol incentives and mining; QVAC emphasizes on-device product suite under Tether.

pros.text: Open incentive design as an alternative to pure SaaS subscription economics.

cons.text: UX, liquidity, and regulatory clarity differ from mainstream SaaS — real integration cost.

entities.name: LM Studio
entities.tagline: GUI-first local LLMs: discover models, chat, ship a localhost API — popular with evaluators.

facts.label: Positioning
facts.value: Closed-source app, free for personal use; strong model browser and one-click runs on Mac/Win/Linux.

facts.label: Vs QVAC
facts.value: Excellent for trying weights quickly — not a vertically integrated Tether product + device agent story.

pros.text: Lowest friction for non-terminal users who still want local inference and OpenAI-compatible localhost serving.

cons.text: Enterprise features (RBAC, audit, SLAs) are not the default story — often paired with other tooling.

entities.name: Apple Intelligence
entities.tagline: Private Cloud Compute + on-device models: Apple’s bundle, not an open agent platform.

facts.label: Scope
facts.value: Tight integration with Apple hardware and OS; selective cloud augmentation with privacy claims.

facts.label: Vs QVAC
facts.value: Different contract: users live inside Apple’s stack — QVAC targets an open local-AI product line under Tether with cross-stack ambition.

pros.text: Massive distribution and polished on-device UX for consumers who stay in the Apple ecosystem.

cons.text: Not a general-purpose programmable agent finance layer — extensibility and rails are platform-governed.
